2.q: K+ uptake of PhaHAK2-n-transformed yeast was decreased only slightly by NaCl, even at concentrations of NaCl as high as 100 mM... the K+/Na+ ratios of the PhaHAK2-n- and PhaHAK2-e-expressing yeasts were not so strongly decreased by increasing NaCl concentrations.

3.q: Within group IV, PhaHAK5-u has 83% identity at the amino acid level with rice OsHAK4... and it has 39% identity with PhaHAK1-u, which belongs to the group I.

4.q: NaCl remarkably decreased the ability of PhaHAK2-u to take up K+... K+ uptake by PhaHAK2-u-transformed yeast decreased by more 50% in the presence of 50 mM NaCl.
